fallback_policy.sort: priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC
fallback_policy.candidate_cap: score at most --candidate-limit ready candidates; 0 means unlimited
fallback_policy.exhaustion_behavior: if scoring evidence is tied or incomplete, preserve fallback rank

issue.acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Cache invalidates on config file changes, routes.jsonl changes, redirects, CLI overrides, and DB path changes.
- No unsafe code and no torn semantic reads; optimistic retry falls back to direct discovery.
- Startup syscall/timing benchmark demonstrates a win before enabling by default.
- Tests cover local, routed, stale, and concurrently-mutated metadata cases.
issue.description: Reduce repeated startup filesystem and config work by adding an explicitly invalidated route/config cache with optimistic version checks. The alien-graveyard inspiration is seqlocks: very cheap reads, retry or fallback on concurrent writes. The Rust implementation should stay safe and simple; no unsafe code. Tests: config mutation invalidation, routed workspace changes, stale-cache fallback, and startup syscall benchmarks.
issue.title: Add optimistic route/config cache for startup metadata

fairness.reason: unassigned work is easiest to allocate fairly
